title: Wales: Elan Valley, Rhayader & St David's

main_route.excerpt: 12.30 Simon & Eve, Tindse leave for farm.
Jam on motorway, lunch, arrive farm at 3. Simon & Eve help bind hop stems around sticks & pull up weeds.
3.45. we reach Polwigo Campsite Llanwrthwl near Rhayader & Elan valley. We go on a lovely walk, small village.

main_route.normalized_name: Porth Clais, St David's, Pembrokeshire, Wales
main_route.excerpt: At 2.30? we set off for St. Davids. We've reached the campsite. Oh dear rain + more rain. Porth Clais
We had to come back to Clanwrthol campsite because Simon ha[d] left coffee pot.
Returning at 2.30, we had tea + a slow drive back to B'ham.

excursions.label: Walk to the Elan Valley reservoirs

stops.excerpt: We set off about 9 to walk over the hills to see the reservoirs in the Elan Valley. Built in 1890's used 50,000 men

excursions.label: Coastal path walk to St David's town

stops.excerpt: We set off along the coastal path - cold damp dreary. ... On to St. David's. Bought bread + chocolate. Lots of people. We had lunch.

excursions.label: St David's Cathedral & walk to St Non's Chapel

stops.excerpt: We wandered round the town & admired the beautiful ceiling & walls of the cathedral. We had lunch there.

stops.normalized_name: St Non's Chapel, St Davids, Pembrokeshire, Wales
stops.excerpt: We came back to the campsite & walked to see St. Non's Chapel ... along the coastal path which again had sheer drops in parts.

excursions.label: Day trip to Dolaucothi Gold Mines

stops.normalized_name: Dolaucothi Gold Mines, Pumsaint, Carmarthenshire, Wales
stops.excerpt: We went on to a Gold Mine stopping at Morrisons on the way. We both enjoyed the gold mine.

excursions.label: Bike ride to Pen y Garreg Dam, Elan Valley

stops.normalized_name: Pen y Garreg Dam, Elan Valley, Powys, Wales
stops.excerpt: We reached Pen y Garreg Dam which holds 1300 mill gall - enough to give B'ham water for 15 days.

unresolved.issue: Overnight stop on the first night (Mon Aug 12) at an unnamed hop farm where the family help with hop work; exact location not given. Reached ~3pm after a motorway jam from Birmingham, departed Tue Aug 13 for Llanwrthwl. Likely in the Welsh Marches / mid-Wales but unlocatable from the text. Kept in main_route as an approximate overnight_base.
unresolved.excerpt: 12.30 Simon & Eve, Tindse leave for farm. Jam on motorway, lunch, arrive farm at 3.

unresolved.issue: A railway viaduct visited on the Saturday drive (after the gold mine, before returning to the Llanwrthwl campsite). Found under scaffolding; not named, so cannot be geolocated. Possibly the Cynghordy Viaduct near Llandovery (on the route between Dolaucothi/Pumsaint and Rhayader), but unconfirmed.
unresolved.excerpt: We drove on to see a bridge railway via duct. It was under scaffolding.

boundary_flags.issue: mixed_start: the opening 'Sunday' entry is the tail of the PREVIOUS trip (driving home, visiting Venetia & David at 'The New House', van milometer passing 100,000, watching Murray in the Wimbledon Final). This trip proper begins at the 'Monday August 12th' heading when 'Simon & Eve, Tinder leave for farm'. Places from the Sunday tail (The New House, Wimbledon, Tufnell Green, Bristol, Witney, Oxford, France) are NOT stops on this trip.

Year discrepancy: trip_id and title say 2016, but the diary heading 'Monday August 12th' and source iso dates indicate 2013 (12 Aug 2013 was a Monday; 12 Aug 2016 was a Friday, consistent with the struck-through 'Friday' in the heading). trip_id retained exactly as supplied; date_start/date_end use 2016 to match the trip_id year but the day-of-week evidence points to 2013.

summary: A late-summer camping trip from Birmingham into mid- and west Wales with Simon, Eve and Tinder the dog. After a first night helping at a hop farm, they based at a campsite in Llanwrthwl near Rhayader to walk the Elan Valley reservoirs, then moved to Porth Clais near St David's for coast-path walks, the cathedral and St Non's Chapel. They returned to the Llanwrthwl campsite, with a day trip to the Dolaucothi Gold Mines and a bike ride to the Pen y Garreg Dam before driving home.
extraction_notes: No sea crossings (Wales trip), so crossings is empty. The trip is effectively a two-base loop: Llanwrthwl (campsite spelled variously 'Polwigo' and 'Clanwrthol') and Porth Clais/St David's, with the unnamed hop farm as the first overnight. Mohne dam, Hamburg, Canada and the family-status mentions (France/Bristol/Tufnell Green/Witney/Oxford) are historical/contextual references, not stops. Salisbury Cathedral appears only as a Constable painting caption (B_0273_R), not a visited place. The railway viaduct (B_0274_L) and the hop farm (B_0270_L) are flagged as unresolved locations. Day-of-week evidence suggests the true year is 2013, not the 2016 in the trip_id (see boundary_flags); dates are recorded under 2016 to match the supplied trip_id with weekday-anchored offsets.
